discussion_comments.comment_body:
About current workflow error at `Print output` task.... 

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/3125580/178809154-e48a3171-5457-455d-aec7-3e3c8070d298.png)

Reading https://www.damirscorner.com/blog/posts/20220506-TokenPermissionsForGithubActions.html... a bit I think that is about GitHub token permissions

See Seth workarround at PR #5557 : https://github.com/SethFalco/free-programming-books/blob/e9f7dff3be87e640a72f97bd4eec1be79ec2b43a/.github/workflows/fpb-lint.yml#L7-L9

My workaround is insecure and shouldn't be used, if I understand correctly.

The real solution is in my comment below. Split it into 2 separate workflows, one creates the message as a file and uploads it as an artifact of the workflow, the other reads the message from that workflow and posts the review.

commented_code:
+name: Comment on the pull request
+
+on:
+  workflow_run:
+    workflows: ["free-programming-books-lint"]
+    types:
+      - completed
+
+env:
+  GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
+
+jobs:
+  upload:

discussion_comments.commented_code:
@@ -0,0 +1,56 @@
+name: Comment on the pull request
+
+on:
+  workflow_run:
+    workflows: ["free-programming-books-lint"]
+    types:
+      - completed
+
+env:
+  GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
+
+jobs:
+  upload:
discussion_comments.comment_body:
```suggestion
  upload:
    permissions:
      pull-requests: write
```

This job would need to explicitly declare write access for `pull-request` here, I think?
Either that, or in the repo settings someone would have to enable write access by default for all workflows, but that's probably not a good idea for a public repo.
